channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: D4vd Collaborations With Laufey, Damiano David, Kali Uchis & Others Pulled From Streaming Services
item.description:
[Eerie music fades out, and the host's voice takes over]
Host: Welcome to our music news update. You might have noticed that several collaborations featuring the artist D4vd have vanished from streaming services. Songs like "Superbloodmoon" with Holly Humberstone, "This Is How It Feels" with Laufey, and "Crashing" with Kali Uchis are all gone.
[Short pause]
Host: This comes after D4vd, whose real name is David Anthony Burke, was charged with first-degree murder. The allegations are connected to the death of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas, whose body was found in the trunk of a Tesla registered to D4vd. According to authorities, Rivas had threatened to expose an inappropriate sexual relationship with D4vd, which allegedly led to her murder.
[Soft music transitions in]
Host: The news has sent shockwaves through the music industry. Kali Uchis had already announced plans to remove "Crashing" from streaming services back in September, after Rivas' body was discovered. Figure-skating champion Alysa Liu also removed the Laufey and D4vd collaboration "This Is How It Feels" from her routine.
[Pause]
Host: D4vd's song "What Are You Waiting For" has also been deleted from the Madden 26 soundtrack, and he was dropped by his label Darkroom/Interscope last year. The only collaboration still available on streaming services is "The Line" with The Kid Laroi.
[Somber music continues]
Host: The Rivas family has released a statement calling for justice, and the Los Angeles County District Attorney has vowed to seek justice for the family. We'll continue to follow this story and provide updates as more information becomes available.
[Music fades out, and the host's voice takes over one last time]
Host: That's all for now. Thank you for tuning in to our music news update.
